text:
Large Language Models (LLMs) have made great progress in automated reasoning and code generation. Unfortunately the use of LLMs in science is limited due to hallucinations and lack of reproducibility (i.e. no verifiable new knowledge generated). In this work we present an evolutionary system called FunSearch (searching in the function space) where a pre-trained LLM is linked to a dedicated and systematic evaluator. Most search algorithms focus on finding the what. Here we aim to find programs which tell us how to solve a problem.

We are excited to announce that FunSearch made the first verifiable scientific discoveries using LLMs for a few of the well-known open problems in computer science. We successfully found new, larger examples for the cap set problem in extremal combinatorics which gives the first improvement to the asymptotic lower bound in 20 years. In addition, FunSearch produced a set of new heuristics for the online bin packing problem that exceed all the existing algorithmic bounds. The output programs are easy to read, short, and scale well for large inputs, while still yielding a clear understanding of what the programs are doing, which opens a window for traditional computer science to also benefit from the discoveries made by FunSearch.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
